title: RHCopywriterBrief
description: Input brief for the Red Hat Copywriter Skill. Defined in PRD-RHCW-001 v0.1 Section 8. This schema is the authoritative machine-readable input contract for the skill.

content_type.description: The format of the content to be generated. Determines routing to the appropriate content-type handler and applies format-specific length constraints.

product_or_topic.description: The Red Hat product, solution, or topic the content covers. Must use approved product names from standards/terminology-list.md on first reference.

target_audience.description: Primary audience persona (e.g. 'IT decision maker', 'DevOps engineer', 'C-suite executive').

key_messages.description: Core messages to convey, in priority order. The skill will incorporate these in sequence, weighting earlier items more heavily.

tone_override.description: Optional tone variant. 'standard' applies default Red Hat voice. Other variants adjust register while remaining within brand guidelines.

word_count_target.description: Optional target word count. Overrides the content_type default length range when provided. Must fall within content_type valid range to avoid validation warning.

call_to_action.description: Optional explicit CTA to embed at the end of the piece (e.g. 'Start your free trial at redhat.com/trial').

messaging_pillars.description: Optional list of Red Hat messaging pillars to align with. When provided, the skill will include a messaging_alignment block in the output indicating which pillars are addressed and any gaps.

_scaffold_note: Schema body is complete per PRD Section 8. Implementation validation logic is out of scope for the design phase.
